rfc_references.title: Zstandard Compression and the application/zstd Media Type

derived_from: RFC 8878 — Zstandard Compression and the application/zstd Media Type (February 2021)
https://github.com/facebook/zstd/blob/dev/lib/zstd.h — public header file (API declarations only, not implementation)
not_derived_from: Any other zstd C implementation source file
provenance.notes: This spec constitutes the clean-room boundary. The implementation team reads this spec only. The spec team read the public documentation listed above. Cross-spec note: the raw content of any zstd-decompressed output can be independently verified using zlib's adler32 checksum against the original plaintext, since both operate on raw byte sequences.

compression_levels.name: ZSTD_CLEVEL_DEFAULT
compression_levels.description: Default compression level; equivalent to level 3 in current implementations

compression_levels.name: ZSTD_minCLevel
compression_levels.description: Minimum compression level (fastest); negative levels exist as ultra-fast modes but level 1 is the minimum for standard API

compression_levels.name: ZSTD_maxCLevel
compression_levels.description: Maximum compression level; corresponds to --ultra mode in the CLI

magic_numbers.name: ZSTD_MAGICNUMBER
magic_numbers.description: Magic number for zstd compressed frames (little-endian in the wire format)

magic_numbers.name: ZSTD_MAGIC_SKIPPABLE_START
magic_numbers.description: First magic number in the skippable frame range; range covers 0x184D2A50–0x184D2A5F

magic_numbers.name: ZSTD_MAGIC_DICTIONARY
magic_numbers.description: Magic number for zstd dictionary files

block_types.name: ZSTD_bt_raw
block_types.description: Raw block: uncompressed literal data stored as-is

block_types.name: ZSTD_bt_rle
block_types.description: RLE block: single repeated byte, size stored separately

block_types.name: ZSTD_bt_compressed
block_types.description: Compressed block: Huffman + FSE encoded literals and sequences

block_types.name: ZSTD_bt_reserved
block_types.description: Reserved; must not appear in valid frames

error_sentinels.name: ZSTD_CONTENTSIZE_UNKNOWN
error_sentinels.description: Returned by ZSTD_getFrameContentSize when content size is not stored in the frame header

error_sentinels.name: ZSTD_CONTENTSIZE_ERROR
error_sentinels.description: Returned by ZSTD_getFrameContentSize when the input is not a valid zstd frame

ZSTD_parameters.description: Aggregate of compression and frame parameters for advanced single-pass API. Not used in simple API but documents the parameter surface.

fields.name: cParams
fields.description: ZSTD_compressionParameters: windowLog, chainLog, hashLog, searchLog, minMatch, targetLength, strategy

fields.name: fParams
fields.description: ZSTD_frameParameters: contentSizeFlag, checksumFlag, noDictIDFlag

ZSTD_inBuffer.description: Input descriptor for streaming API. Points into a caller-managed buffer.

fields.name: src
fields.description: Pointer to start of input buffer

fields.name: size
fields.description: Total size of the input buffer in bytes

fields.name: pos
fields.description: Current read position within src; advanced by the library on each call

ZSTD_outBuffer.description: Output descriptor for streaming API. Points into a caller-managed buffer.

fields.name: dst
fields.description: Pointer to start of output buffer

fields.name: size
fields.description: Total capacity of the output buffer in bytes

fields.name: pos
fields.description: Current write position within dst; advanced by the library on each call

ZSTD_versionString.description: Returns the version string of the zstd library currently loaded at runtime. Useful for display and runtime version checks.
postconditions: Return value starts with '1.' for all 1.x releases

ZSTD_versionNumber.description: Returns the version number as an integer: major*100*100 + minor*100 + release. For version 1.5.7 returns 10507.

return_values.positive: Encoded version integer: major*10000 + minor*100 + release

ZSTD_maxCLevel.description: Returns the maximum compression level supported by this build of zstd. Compression levels range from 1 to this value. Passing a higher level to ZSTD_compress is undefined behavior.

return_values.22: Current maximum compression level (as of zstd 1.4.x and later)

postconditions: Return value >= 22 for all builds covering spec_for_versions

ZSTD_compressBound.description: Returns an upper bound on the compressed output size for an input of srcSize bytes. Always call this to size the destination buffer before calling ZSTD_compress. Never returns an error code.

parameters.name: srcSize
parameters.description: Expected uncompressed input size in bytes

return_values.positive: Upper bound in bytes; guaranteed >= actual compressed output for any input of the given size

postconditions: Return value > srcSize for any srcSize > 0 (compressed bound always exceeds raw size due to frame overhead)
Return value is never an error code (ZSTD_isError returns 0 for this value)

ZSTD_compress.description: Single-call compression. Compresses src into dst at the given compressionLevel. dst must be at least ZSTD_compressBound(srcSize) bytes.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: Output buffer; must be at least ZSTD_compressBound(srcSize) bytes

parameters.name: dstCapacity
parameters.description: Size of the output buffer in bytes

parameters.name: compressionLevel
parameters.description: Compression level: 1 (fastest) to ZSTD_maxCLevel() (best ratio). Use 3 for default.

return_values.positive: Number of bytes written to dst (the compressed size)
return_values.error_code: An error code that ZSTD_isError returns 1 for; e.g. if dstCapacity is too small

postconditions: Output can be decompressed by ZSTD_decompress to recover the original bytes exactly

ZSTD_decompress.description: Single-call decompression. Caller must supply an output buffer large enough for the uncompressed result. If the original size was stored in the frame header (the default), use ZSTD_getFrameContentSize to determine the required buffer size.

parameters.name: dstCapacity
parameters.description: Size of the output buffer in bytes

parameters.name: compressedSize
parameters.description: Exact size of the compressed frame; must not include trailing garbage

return_values.positive: Number of bytes written to dst (the uncompressed size)
return_values.error_code: An error code that ZSTD_isError returns 1 for; e.g. if dst is too small or src is corrupt

preconditions: src must point to a valid, complete zstd frame
dstCapacity must be >= the actual uncompressed content size
postconditions: Output bytes exactly match the original plaintext that was compressed

ZSTD_isError.description: Tests whether a size_t return value represents an error code. zstd encodes errors as large size_t values near SIZE_MAX. Use this after every call to ZSTD_compress, ZSTD_decompress, or any streaming function.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: A return value from any zstd function

return_values.1: code is an error; call ZSTD_getErrorName(code) for a description

postconditions: ZSTD_isError(0) == 0 — zero is never an error
ZSTD_isError(ZSTD_compressBound(n)) == 0 for any n — compressBound never returns an error code

ZSTD_getErrorName.description: Maps a zstd error code to a human-readable string. Safe to call with any size_t value, including non-error values.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: A return value from any zstd function

return_values.non_null: Null-terminated ASCII description, e.g. 'No error' or 'Destination buffer is too small'

postconditions: For non-error codes, returns a string describing the operation (e.g. 'No error')

ZSTD_getFrameContentSize.description: Reads the uncompressed content size stored in the frame header. ZSTD_compress stores it by default. Use the result to size the decompression output buffer.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: Pointer to the start of a zstd frame

parameters.name: srcSize
parameters.description: Number of bytes available at src; must cover at least the frame header

return_values.ZSTD_CONTENTSIZE_UNKNOWN(-1): Content size not stored in the frame header; must use streaming decompression or over-allocate
return_values.ZSTD_CONTENTSIZE_ERROR(-2): src does not begin with a valid zstd frame magic number

invariants.description: ZSTD_versionString() must return a non-NULL string starting with '1.' for all 1.x releases covered by this spec
ZSTD_maxCLevel() must return 22 for all zstd builds in spec range
ZSTD_isError(0) must return 0 — zero is never an error code in the zstd API
ZSTD_isError(1) must return 0 — small positive values are valid compressed sizes, not errors
ZSTD_compressBound(35) must return a value >= 36 — the compressed bound always exceeds the input size due to frame overhead
ZSTD_compressBound(35) must return exactly 98 — the documented upper bound for a 35-byte input
ZSTD_compressBound(0) must return a value > 0 — even an empty input incurs frame overhead
ZSTD_isError(ZSTD_compressBound(35)) must return 0 — compressBound never produces an error code
ZSTD_compressBound(1048576) must return a value >= 1048577 — bound always exceeds source size
ZSTD_versionNumber() must return a value >= 10400 (encoding version 1.4.0) for the spec range
ZSTD_isError for a known zstd error sentinel (SIZE_MAX - 1 cast as size_t) must return 1 — large values near SIZE_MAX are error codes
ZSTD_isError(ZSTD_maxCLevel()) must return 0 — the max compression level integer is not an error code
ZSTD_compressBound(1024) must return a value >= ZSTD_compressBound(35) — bound is monotonically non-decreasing with input size
ZSTD_isError(ZSTD_versionNumber()) must return 0 — the version integer is not in the error range
ZSTD_compressBound(256) must return a value >= 257 and be free of error

zstd_frame.description: zstd compressed frame format. All multi-byte integers are little-endian unless otherwise noted. A complete zstd stream consists of one or more frames. The standard compressed frame begins with a 4-byte magic number and a variable-length Frame_Header.

fields.name: Magic_Number
fields.description: Frame magic number, little-endian. Always 0xFD2FB528. Bytes on wire: 0x28 0xB5 0x2F 0xFD.

fields.name: Frame_Header_Descriptor
fields.description: One byte encoding FCS_Field_Size (bits 7-6), Single_Segment_Flag (bit 5), Content_Checksum_Flag (bit 2), DictID_Flag (bits 1-0). Controls which optional Frame_Header fields are present.

fields.name: Window_Descriptor
fields.description: Present only when Single_Segment_Flag == 0. Encodes the minimum decompressor window size as Mantissa * 2^(Exponent+10). High 3 bits = Exponent, low 5 bits = Mantissa.

fields.name: Dictionary_ID
fields.description: Present only when DictID_Flag != 0. Size is 0, 1, 2, or 4 bytes as indicated by DictID_Flag. Identifies the dictionary required for decompression.

fields.name: Frame_Content_Size
fields.description: Present only when FCS_Field_Size != 0 (or when Single_Segment_Flag == 1). Size is 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes as indicated by FCS_Field_Size. Stores the uncompressed content size. ZSTD_compress always stores this field.

fields.name: Data_Block_Header
fields.description: 3-byte block header. Bits 0: Last_Block flag (1 = this is the last block). Bits 2-1: Block_Type (0=Raw, 1=RLE, 2=Compressed, 3=Reserved). Bits 23-3: Block_Size in bytes.

fields.name: Block_Data
fields.description: Block payload. For Raw blocks: literal bytes. For RLE blocks: a single byte repeated Block_Size times. For Compressed blocks: Huffman-coded literals section followed by FSE-coded sequences section.

fields.name: Content_Checksum
fields.description: Present only when Content_Checksum_Flag == 1 in the Frame_Header_Descriptor. XXHash64 of the decompressed content, truncated to the low 32 bits, stored little-endian. ZSTD_compress does not store this by default (use advanced API to enable).

zstd_skippable_frame.description: Skippable frame format. Used to embed arbitrary metadata in a zstd stream without affecting decompression. Any magic number in the range 0x184D2A50–0x184D2A5F identifies a skippable frame.

fields.name: Magic_Number
fields.description: Skippable frame magic number. Any value in the range 0x184D2A50 to 0x184D2A5F (inclusive) is valid.

fields.name: Frame_Size
fields.description: Size of the User_Data field in bytes, little-endian. May be zero.

fields.name: User_Data
fields.description: Arbitrary application data of Frame_Size bytes. Decompressors must skip this frame entirely.

zstd_block_compressed.description: Layout of a Compressed block payload (Block_Type == 2). Consists of a Literals Section followed by a Sequences Section. Both sections use entropy coding (Huffman for literals, FSE for sequence codes).

fields.name: Literals_Section_Header
fields.description: 1–5 bytes encoding Literals_Block_Type (bits 1-0: Raw=0, RLE=1, Compressed=2, Treeless=3) and the compressed/regenerated size of the literals.

fields.name: Huffman_Tree_Description
fields.description: Present when Literals_Block_Type == Compressed and a new tree is defined. Encodes the Huffman tree used to decode the literals stream.

fields.name: Literals_Stream
fields.description: Huffman-coded (or raw/RLE) literal bytes.

fields.name: Sequences_Section_Header
fields.description: 1–3 bytes. First byte encodes the sequence count: 0 means no sequences (literals only). Then per-symbol-type flags for Literals_Lengths, Offsets, Match_Lengths FSE tables.

fields.name: FSE_Tables
fields.description: Optional FSE table descriptions for Literals_Lengths, Offsets, and Match_Lengths symbol types.

fields.name: Sequences_Bitstream
fields.description: FSE-coded sequence commands. Each sequence encodes a literal length, a match offset, and a match length used to reconstruct the original data.

error_model.return_code_semantics: zstd functions that return size_t encode errors as large values near SIZE_MAX. Specifically, an error code E satisfies: E > (SIZE_MAX / 2). Use ZSTD_isError(code) to test for errors. Non-error return values are valid sizes and are always in the range [0, SIZE_MAX/2]. This is distinct from zlib's signed-integer error convention.
error_model.error_stickiness: For streaming contexts (ZSTD_CCtx, ZSTD_DCtx), once an error is returned the context is in an unspecified state and must be reset with ZSTD_CCtx_reset or ZSTD_DCtx_reset before reuse. Single-call functions (ZSTD_compress, ZSTD_decompress) do not maintain persistent state, so each call is independent.

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_no_error
error_codes.meaning: Operation completed successfully; return value is a valid size

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_GENERIC
error_codes.meaning: Unspecified generic error

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_prefix_unknown
error_codes.meaning: Source data does not begin with a valid zstd or skippable frame magic number

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_version_unsupported
error_codes.meaning: Frame was produced by a newer version of zstd and uses features not supported by this build

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_frameParameter_unsupported
error_codes.meaning: Frame header contains a parameter value not supported by this implementation

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_frameParameter_windowTooLarge
error_codes.meaning: Frame requires a decompression window larger than ZSTD_MAXWINDOWSIZE

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_corruption_detected
error_codes.meaning: Compressed data is corrupt: invalid block type, bad checksum, or truncated stream

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_checksum_wrong
error_codes.meaning: Content checksum mismatch: frame checksum does not match decompressed content (only when Content_Checksum_Flag was set)

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_literals_headerWrong
error_codes.meaning: Literals section header is invalid

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_dictionary_corrupted
error_codes.meaning: The dictionary file is corrupt or has an invalid magic number

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_dictionary_wrong
error_codes.meaning: Frame was compressed with a different dictionary than what was provided for decompression

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_dictionaryCreation_failed
error_codes.meaning: Dictionary training or creation failed

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_parameter_unsupported
error_codes.meaning: A compression or decompression parameter value is outside the supported range

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_parameter_combination_unsupported
error_codes.meaning: A combination of parameters is not supported together

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_parameter_outOfBound
error_codes.meaning: A parameter value is outside its valid bounds

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_tableLog_tooLarge
error_codes.meaning: FSE table log is too large for this build

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_maxSymbolValue_tooLarge
error_codes.meaning: Symbol value exceeds the supported maximum in an FSE or Huffman table

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_maxSymbolValue_tooSmall
error_codes.meaning: Maximum symbol value is too small for the data being compressed

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_stabilityCondition_notRespected
error_codes.meaning: Input or output buffer was moved or resized during an in-place operation

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_stage_wrong
error_codes.meaning: Function called at the wrong stage of the streaming API lifecycle

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_init_missing
error_codes.meaning: Context was not initialized before use

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_memory_allocation
error_codes.meaning: Memory allocation failed (malloc returned NULL)

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_workSpace_tooSmall
error_codes.meaning: Provided static workspace is too small for the requested operation

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_dstSize_tooSmall
error_codes.meaning: Destination buffer is too small to hold the compressed or decompressed output

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_srcSize_wrong
error_codes.meaning: Source size declared in the frame header does not match the actual amount of data provided

error_codes.name: ZSTD_error_dstBuffer_null
error_codes.meaning: Destination buffer pointer is NULL but dstCapacity > 0
